sources.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
sources.exact_document_title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
sources.version: 2014 update; current AAO-HNSF acute otitis externa guideline
sources.population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, emergency, paediatric, internal-medicine, and otolaryngology settings.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for diffuse acute otitis externa symptoms, modifiers, and key actions; partial for broad ear pain and not validated for UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

exact_sections.heading: Fact sheet — significant guideline action statements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

archive.title: Updated Clinical Practice Guideline: Acute Otitis Externa — Official Fact Sheet and Key Action Statements
archive.publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
archive.population: People aged 2 years or older with diffuse acute otitis externa.
archive.setting: Primary, emergency, paediatric, internal-medicine, and otolaryngology settings.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Fact sheet — AOE symptoms and modifying factors
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Fact sheet — significant guideline action statements
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports distinguishing AOE from other otalgia or otorrhoea, pain assessment, clinician-selected topical management, administration discussion, and reassessment after non-response.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
sources.clinical_setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for acute otitis media; partial for a broad pediatric ear-pain workflow and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.12–1.1.14
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.

archive.title: Otitis media (acute): antimicrobial prescribing — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.population: Children and young people with acute otitis media.
archive.setting: Health services caring for children and young people with acute otitis media.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.7
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.12–1.1.14
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Requires review for rapid worsening or systemic illness and identifies severe infection and acute complications including mastoiditis for hospital assessment.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Hearing loss in adults: assessment and management — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE NG98; last updated and reviewed 2023-10-02; minor link update 2025-03
sources.population: Adults aged over 18 presenting with hearing difficulties or hearing loss.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary, community, audiology, and secondary care.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult hearing-difficulty assessment, red-flag referral, otoscopy, audiology, and hearing-support documentation; partial for children and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — initial, sudden, and urgent assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports first-presentation hearing assessment, exclusion of wax or acute infection, audiology, sudden or rapid loss, unilateral neurological signs, otalgia, otorrhoea, and immunocompromise review.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.3.2 — additional symptoms, ear findings, and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.8.1 — audiology assessment, management, and support
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.
